report.hypothesis: Introducing a bulky aromatic residue at position 5 and a hydrophobic residue at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/5.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is achievable, but se

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is achievable, but selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5 requires focused mutation of non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration should prioritize these mutations to push Δmargin further into the positive range.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved hydrophobic or electrostatic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the FWKT(7-10) pharmacophore or Cys3/Cys14 disulfide.
